items.title: Immigration enforcement surge begins in Charlotte, North Carolina
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>People rally against federal immigration enforcement after agents were seen making arrests in multiple locations across Charlotte. (AP video: Erik Verduzco) 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Sting: 'I'm not just a rock star. I'm more than that'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Reflecting on his return to “The Last Ship,” Sting says the story is key to understanding himself. He shared the insight after a short set at the Metropolitan Opera House, ahead of next year’s residency.

#sting 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Venezuela's Maduro sings John Lennon's 'Imagine' as he talks about US tensions
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The president of Venezuela took part in an event with his supporters on Saturday, where he recalled British musician John Lennon and even went on to sing part of one of his iconic songs, “Imagine.” Maduro’s public appearance and his comments about seeking peace come as tensions with the U.S. rise amid the presence of warships in the Caribbean near Venezuela’s coast, which the Trump administration says are aimed at fighting drug trafficking. (AP Video by Andry Rincón)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Dotting the I defines Ohio State and its band
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At 89 years old, Script Ohio not only remains one of the greatest traditions in college football, but also serves as a signature for Ohio State University and the entire state.

#ohiostate #ohiostateuniversity #scriptohio</article>

items.title: Man films Border Patrol agents smashing his car window in Charlotte
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Willy Aceituno, a Honduran-born U.S. citizen, was stopped twice by Border Patrol agents in Charlotte, North Carolina. During the second encounter, they forced him from his vehicle after breaking the window and threw him to the ground.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: 4 law enforcement officers shot in rural Kansas
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Four officers were shot while responding to a rural residence south of Topeka, Kansas. A male suspect died from gunshot wounds and another person was injured and taken to a hospital, according to Kansas Bureau of Investigation and state Highway Patrol.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Jesse Eisenberg says he doesn't 'watch any movies I've been in'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Returning for the third "Now You See Me" film, Jesse Eisenberg said he loves making movies but will not watch them because he doesn't "need to see what I look like from the side." 

#jesseeisenberg 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: POV: You're dotting the I in 'Script Ohio'
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Sousaphone player Brayden Hyder takes you along for Ohio State University Marching Band's tradition of dotting the i to complete “Script Ohio.” 

#ohiostateuniversity #ohiostate #scriptohio</article>

items.title: Climate protesters demand to be heard at COP30 in Brazil
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of demonstrators marched in Belem, Brazil, at the halfway point of the United Nations climate talks, demanding progress from negotiators on climate change. (AP Video by Joshua A. Bickel, Teresa de Miguel and Andre Penner. Produced by Joshua A. Bickel)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Storm Claudia causes widespread flooding in Wales and England
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Storm Claudia had also triggered an amber weather warning for rain in parts of the UK on Saturday. Emergency services carried out evacuations, welfare checks and rescues in the early hours of Saturday morning, according to the South Wales Fire and Rescue Service.

Picture credit: Ben Birchall/PA 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: El Salvador president launches app for free medical consultations via mobile devices
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Salvadorian President Nayib Bukele launched Thursday an AI powered app to provide free medical consultations on mobile devices. Bukele presented the initiative on national television accompanied by representatives from Google and the Development Bank of Latin America and the Caribbean (CAF).

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.content:

items.title: Venezuela's Maduro issues appeal for peace to people of United States
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The president warned that the naval presence — more than a dozen ships and 12,000 troops led by the aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford — could foreshadow the "surge and imposition of a military and colonial trend." (AP Video by Andry Rincon)

items.title: Here's how COP30 affects you
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Global leaders gathered in Brazil for COP30 to discuss ways to reduce global warming. Here’s how that can impact your life.</article>

items.title: Bills' Damar Hamlin says he was built to handle adversity even before cardiac arrest
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>In this episode of On Football with Rob Maaddi, Bills safety Damar Hamlin talks about overcoming a cardiac arrest on the field nearly three years ago, how he’s helping his teammates now while sidelined with another injury and his upcoming role in a Christmas movie. Plus, a four-pack of Pro Picks for Week 11.</article>
